purpose: Classify remaining Python-owned behavior after contract, schema, parser, policy, and template extraction so runtime code is intentional implementation rather than hidden contract authority.

boundaries.why_python_owned: Lifecycle commands perform filesystem writes, dry-run planning, conservative reconciliation, local-only uninstall behavior, and preflight-gated mutation checks that depend on current repo state.

Reports combine live module detection, config layering, installed-surface checks, integrity checks, and current planning state; the operation contracts describe observable behavior without freezing repo inspection logic as static data.
The report router is derived rendering over live report payloads and contract-backed sections; cli.py owns command wiring while reporting_support owns compact router shaping.
Token parsing, freshness checks, current time comparison, and strict-gate decisions are runtime safety behavior; static policy values are already contract-backed.
The parser and dispatch remain runtime adapter code, while command names, options, option groups, and executable operation coverage are manifest-backed and checked for parity.
Path normalization, fnmatch ordering, package-boundary detection, and attention-path selection depend on caller input and the current checkout; stable proof and context fragments are contract-backed.

remaining_extractable_content.summary: Authority marker policy is still table-like data inside cli.py and remains the next low-risk extraction target after schema selection.

boundaries.why_python_owned: Parser diagnostics, TOML/BOM handling, local override layering, and typed runtime objects are implementation behavior; reusable shapes are schema-backed.

Large Markdown adapter templates still render from Python for now; the stable start/implement context fragments have moved to context_templates.json, and the remaining adapter-template extraction is tracked separately.

remaining_extractable_content.summary: Extract the root AGENTS adapter template so maintainer-surface refresh drift is easier to detect and fix.

root_cli_authority_audit.rule: Direct root CLI edits are acceptable for runtime primitives and live inspection, but command/interface authority should be contract-backed, generated, or tracked as an extraction/guard candidate.

responsibility_classes.route_when: The behavior performs live repo inspection, subprocess execution, mutation sequencing, preflight token evaluation, or output emission mechanics.
responsibility_classes.extract_or_guard_when: Stable command shape, effect hints, schemas, or reusable wording can be expressed in contracts without owning live behavior.

responsibility_classes.allowed_owner: src/agentic_workspace/_runtime_cli.py as renderer over contract and live module payloads
responsibility_classes.route_when: The output assembles current config, module reports, planning state, or contract manifests into a compact answer.
responsibility_classes.extract_or_guard_when: A rendered field becomes stable enough to require schema, operation contract, or report-contract coverage.

responsibility_classes.route_when: Parser/help/dispatch wiring is already backed by command_adapter_generation.json, command_package_ir.json, operation contracts, and conformance refs.
responsibility_classes.extract_or_guard_when: A direct CLI edit changes command identity, option semantics, effect hints, schema refs, or generated adapter metadata.

responsibility_classes.allowed_owner: contracts, schemas, command-package IR, or explicit follow-on issue
responsibility_classes.route_when: The concern is visible command shape, option semantics, stable help/source wording, or reusable interface policy still encoded in root CLI code.
responsibility_classes.extract_or_guard_when: The same shape must be consumed by generated packages, non-Python adapters, weak-agent routing, or contract checks.

current_audit.evidence: install/init/upgrade/uninstall perform dry-run planning, conservative writes, deletion safeguards, local-only preservation, and preflight-gated mutation.
current_audit.next_review: Keep lifecycle command shape and effect hints contract-backed; do not extract mutation primitives without a dedicated lifecycle generation slice.

current_audit.evidence: report/status/doctor combine live module detection, resolved config, installed-surface checks, and planning summaries; report-router shaping lives in agentic_workspace.reporting_support.
current_audit.next_review: Keep stable report sections covered by report contracts and keep cli.py limited to command wiring plus live payload assembly.

current_audit.evidence: cli_commands.json, cli_option_groups.json, command_adapter_generation.json, generated/python/*/generated_command_adapters.json, and command package checks cover selected command shapes.
current_audit.next_review: Direct parser changes must prove they are runtime glue or update the command contracts and generated adapters.

current_audit.evidence: setup, prompt, skills, system-intent, and note-delegation-outcome still have root CLI interface shape or guidance behavior that is not fully represented as generated adapter metadata.
current_audit.next_review: Select the next read-only support command for command_adapter_generation or add a guard that keeps direct edits classified.

next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.why: Support commands are the next visible root CLI surfaces whose command/interface semantics can move out of cli.py without touching runtime primitives.
next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.provenance: Closed historical audit work identified this candidate; it is not the live owner for future extraction work.

next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.why: New stable report sections should be reviewed as report contract/schema authority instead of becoming hidden interface authority in cli.py.
next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.provenance: Closed historical audit work identified this candidate; it is not the live owner for future guard work.

next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.why: Planning and memory package support commands need the same interface-authority routing before broad non-Python generation expands.
next_extraction_or_guard_candidates.provenance: Closed historical audit work identified this candidate; it is not the live owner for future package support-command work.

allowed_when: runtime primitive implementation or live workspace inspection changes
dispatch glue while a command is not yet covered by generated adapters
urgent migration exceptions with proof output naming why definitions were insufficient
route_to_contract_when: visible command names, option semantics, help text source, effect hints, schema refs, conformance refs, or generated package metadata change
a stable report/defaults field becomes cross-adapter authority
a direct edit would be repeated by another generated target
review_requires: agentic-workspace proof direct_cli_edit_review for any src/agentic_workspace/_runtime_cli.py change
root_cli_authority audit check when the edit changes interface or report authority
command generation or contract-surface validation when command semantics change
